top_candidates.reason: Keep-rate, semantic satisfaction signals, and an order-of-magnitude drop in unexpected tool-call errors make this a concrete harness-improvement playbook.
Real-session tasks plus versioned refreshes offer a practical replacement for increasingly saturated public coding benchmarks.
The audit quantifies how flawed tests and training-data exposure can turn a popular coding benchmark into false confidence.
Runtime controls, dataset gating, and trace review emerge as the practical defenses for web-enabled eval integrity.
Production routing data shows a real workload split between cheap-volume models and expensive frontier coding paths.
